title: Charmed Life - Chapter 18 (Fragment 5)
text:
William Nostrum was shouting to each group who hurried through, “Spread out. Spread out up the meadow. Surround the gate there! Leave no avenue of escape.”

Janet forced her way among them and seized Cat’s arm. “Cat! What have we done? Don’t tell me these aren’t all witches and warlocks, because I won’t believe you!”

“Ah, my dear Gwendolen!” said Mr. Henry Nostrum. “Plan Two is under way.”

By this time, the sloping sides of the meadow were crowded with witches and warlocks. The ground quivered to their trampling and buzzed with their cheerful conversation. There were hundreds of them—a nodding of garish hats and shiny toppers, like the audience at the opening of a bazaar.

As soon as the last necromancer had hurried between the pillars, Henry Nostrum put a heavy possessive hand on Cat’s shoulder. Cat wondered uneasily whether it was just an accident that it was the same hand which held his postcard to Mrs. Sharp. He saw that the Willing Warlock had stationed himself by one of the broken pillars, blue-chinned and cheerful as ever in his tight Sunday suit. Mr. William Nostrum had put as much of himself as would go behind the other pillar and, for some reason, he had taken off his heavy silver watch-chain and was swinging it in one hand.

“Now, my dear Gwendolen,” said Henry Nostrum, “would you care for the honor of summoning Chrestomanci?”

“I—I’d rather not,” said Janet.

“Then I’ll take it upon myself,” said Henry Nostrum, perfectly well pleased. He cleared his throat and shouted in a fluting tenor, “Chrestomanci! Chrestomanci! Come to me.”

And Chrestomanci was standing between the pillars.

Chrestomanci must have been on his way up the avenue from church. He had his tall gray hat in one hand and, with the other, he was in the act of putting his prayer book into the pocket of his beautiful dove-gray coat. The assembled witches and necromancers greeted him with a sort of groaning sigh. Chrestomanci blinked around at them, in his mildest and most bewildered way. He became even vaguer and more bewildered when he happened to see Cat and Janet.

Cat opened his mouth to shout at Chrestomanci to go away. But the Willing Warlock leaped on Chrestomanci the moment he appeared. He was growling. His fingernails were growing into claws and his teeth into fangs.

Chrestomanci stuffed the prayer book into his pocket and turned his vague look on the Willing Warlock. The Willing Warlock stood still in midair and shrank. He shrank so fast, he made a whirring sound. Then he was a small brown caterpillar. He dropped to the grass and wriggled there. But, while he was still shrinking, William Nostrum pounced out from behind the other pillar and deftly wrapped his watch-chain around Chrestomanci’s right hand.

“Behind you!” shrieked Cat and Janet, too late.

After barely one wriggle, the caterpillar burst up out of the grass and became the Willing Warlock again, a little disheveled, but very pleased with himself. He threw himself on Chrestomanci again. As for Chrestomanci, it was plain that the watch-chain had somehow disabled him completely. There was a second or so of furious struggle in the archway, while the Willing Warlock tried to grab Chrestomanci in both brawny arms, and Chrestomanci tried to get the watch-chain off his wrist using his left hand, and William Nostrum hung on to it fiercely. None of them used any magic, and Chrestomanci seemed only able to shoulder the Willing Warlock weakly aside. After two attempts, the Willing Warlock wrapped his arms around Chrestomanci from behind and William Nostrum dragged a pair of silver handcuffs from his pocket and snapped them on both Chrestomanci’s wrists.

vocabulary.definition: To bring together in one place for a common purpose.
vocabulary.example: The assembled witches and necromancers greeted him.

vocabulary.definition: A route or means by which someone can escape from a dangerous or difficult situation.
vocabulary.example: Leave no avenue of escape.

vocabulary.definition: A market, especially in the Middle East, consisting of a street lined with shops.
vocabulary.example: Like the audience at the opening of a bazaar.

vocabulary.definition: Confused or perplexed.
vocabulary.example: Chrestomanci blinked around at them in his most bewildered way.

vocabulary.definition: Physically strong and muscular.
vocabulary.example: Grab Chrestomanci in both brawny arms.

vocabulary.definition: A sharp curved nail on the foot of an animal.
vocabulary.example: His fingernails were growing into claws.

vocabulary.definition: In a way that is skillful and quick in movement.
vocabulary.example: William Nostrum pounced out and deftly wrapped his watch-chain.

vocabulary.definition: To skillfully and quickly encircle or cover with something.
vocabulary.example: Deftly wrapped his watch-chain around Chrestomanci’s hand.

vocabulary.definition: To make something unable to work or operate.
vocabulary.example: The watch-chain had somehow disabled him completely.

vocabulary.definition: Untidy or disordered in appearance.
vocabulary.example: A little dishevelled, but very pleased with himself.

vocabulary.definition: A long pointed tooth especially of a dog or wolf.
vocabulary.example: His teeth into fangs.

vocabulary.definition: In a violent, intense, or determined way.
vocabulary.example: William Nostrum hung on to it fiercely.

vocabulary.definition: A clear, melodious and high-pitched male singing voice or speaking tone resembling a flute.
vocabulary.example: He cleared his throat and shouted in a fluting tenor.

vocabulary.definition: Obtrusively bright and showy; lurid.
vocabulary.example: A nodding of garish hats and shiny toppers.

vocabulary.definition: A deep sound expressing disappointment, pain, or longing.
vocabulary.example: Greeted him with a sort of groaning sigh.

vocabulary.definition: To make a low guttural sound of anger or hostility.
vocabulary.example: He was growling.

vocabulary.definition: A pair of metal rings joined by a chain, used for fastening a prisoner's wrists together.
vocabulary.example: William Nostrum dragged a pair of silver handcuffs from his pocket.

vocabulary.definition: To jump or spring a long way, to a great height, or with great force.
vocabulary.example: The Willing Warlock leaped on Chrestomanci.

vocabulary.definition: A person who practices necromancy; a wizard or magician who communicates with the dead to predict the future.
vocabulary.example: As soon as the last necromancer had hurried between the pillars.

vocabulary.definition: Demanding someone’s total attention and love, or keeping control of something and preventing its use by others.
vocabulary.example: Put a heavy possessive hand on Cat’s shoulder.

vocabulary.definition: To jump or spring suddenly to catch or seize something.
vocabulary.example: William Nostrum pounced out from behind the pillar.

vocabulary.definition: To tremble or shake with a slight rapid motion.
vocabulary.example: The ground quivered to their trampling.

vocabulary.definition: To utter a high-pitched piercing sound or words, especially as an expression of terror, pain, or excitement.
vocabulary.example: ‘Behind you!’ shrieked Cat.

vocabulary.definition: To position oneself in a particular place.
vocabulary.example: The Willing Warlock had stationed himself by one of the broken pillars.

vocabulary.definition: To enclose on all sides; to encircle someone or something.
vocabulary.example: Spread out up the meadow. Surround the gate there!

vocabulary.definition: In a way that shows discomfort or anxiety.
vocabulary.example: Cat wondered uneasily whether it was just an accident.

vocabulary.definition: Not clearly expressed, defined, or understood.
vocabulary.example: He became even vaguer and more bewildered.

vocabulary.definition: Making a low, continuous, regular sound like that of a machine or flying insect.
vocabulary.example: He shrank so fast, he made a whirring sound.
